notes: Daily vignettes — small flavor beats that fire on each non-Sunday day so the day loop has texture between dispatch decisions. Each beat is 1-3 sentences. The engine picks one weighted by region visibility + day-range eligibility + the player's current state. Beats with one_shot:true never repeat once fired. Beats without one_shot can recur (the pool is sampled with weight to avoid same-beat-in-a-row). Author voice: present-tense, second person sparingly, mostly third person observation. The vignettes shape the rhythm — half of them are about Graustark's everyday running, half are about the regions at the edges. Some have a downstream consequence as a follow-up vignette one day later.

vignettes.body: Tuesday morning, 6:42. The cathedral office is opened. The mail under the door is two flyers, a parish newsletter, and a postcard from Pancho's in Lubbock that just says THANKS.
Faith II stops by at 11:14 with a paper cup of coffee from the storefront. She is wearing the cap she always wears. She does not, this morning, say anything.
JF leaves a note on the cathedral office door at 6:50: BACK BY 2, ON THE BOILER. The note is written on the back of a receipt for two pounds of marrow.
The cathedral bell pulls at 11. Three of the regulars are inside before the second pull. The fourth is on the bench out back with a paper cup of the same coffee.
Hector's kitchen door is propped open with a brick. The brick has been the prop for the brick's whole career. The kitchen smells like cardamom and andouille and the wax he uses on the prep table.
The storefront's payphone rings twice and stops. T. doesn't answer. He knows whose ring that is.

On the levee road at noon, the county camera at the second-to-last house is still showing the same green frame. The bait shop is still the bait shop. The dog at the end of the bait shop's porch is asleep.
Mom calls the office line at 4:18. She doesn't say why. She talks about a recipe she is going to make Thursday. Frasier listens. The recipe is not the thing she called about.
The post-office at Ninth and the church loses power for 18 minutes. The clerk apologizes to the line. The line is two people, both of whom understand.

Jules opens the timber-yard office at 6:42 AM Pacific. He makes the coffee. He reads the gazette. He writes a note about the dirt road north of the yard and pins it to the corkboard. The note is for himself.
The diner on the highway in Small Wood is a slow Tuesday. The cook is sixty-four. The waitress is his niece. She pours Jules's coffee without asking. He does not, this Tuesday, talk.
The cypress at the southwest corner of Small Wood looks the same today as it did yesterday. The table is dropping. The cypress is doing what the cypress is doing.
Phase Three's surveyor is in Harmony Creek for a Tuesday meeting that runs 14 minutes. He brings printed maps. The HOA has the same printed maps. Everyone nods.
Mrs. Salinas walks the cul-de-sac at the end of phase two at 7:08 PM. She walks counter-clockwise. She walks in silence. Two porches' porch lights come on as she passes; one stays off.
The model home's foundation crack is one-eighth of an inch wider than the last measurement. The realtor still has Sunday open-house signs in the trunk.
An envelope arrives at the cathedral office through the mail slot. No return address. Inside: a Polaroid of the riverfront in 1989 with Pop in the foreground. Nobody owns up.
Sammy walks past the cathedral office at dusk. He doesn't stop. He doesn't wave. He carries something wrapped in newspaper. The newspaper is the Sunday Picayune.
Mackenzie's car is parked at the storefront when Frasier walks by at lunch. She is in the booth across from booth four. She is reading. She does not look up.
STEAMBOAT_72 runs the boat down the channel in Harmony Creek at 3:18 PM. He waves at the porch at the second-to-last house. He gets a wave back. He does not slow down.
The boiler in the cathedral basement hums two semitones flat. JF knows. JF will fix it. JF says not yet.
Frasier reads three pages of the Pliny at 7:30 AM. He reads the same three pages he was reading yesterday. He does not, today, get to page four.
The cathedral office's clock runs three minutes fast. It has run three minutes fast since 1988. Pop set it that way on purpose. Frasier has never reset it.
A stray dog sits at the cathedral side door at 5 PM. It is not a stray. It belongs to the bait shop man. It has, this summer, become the cathedral's dog too.
Hector tests Friday's andouille on Wednesday at 2 PM. He tests it on Faith II, who tells him it is the best he has made. Hector says she is lying. Faith II tells him to make it again on Friday.
Aria draws a shape on the back of the church bulletin at the 11 AM mass. Nicola does not look at the drawing. She does not have to. She knows the shape.
